sourceMovie.title: A Perfect Murder
sourceMovie.whyPeopleLoveIt: People revisit A Perfect Murder for the same reason it broke through in 1998: clear stakes, memorable faces, and a thriller rhythm that doesn't stall in the middle. Andrew Davis keeps the ensemble pointed in one direction. The crime and thriller blend is the engine: it lets the film shift registers without feeling like two different movies stitched together.

recommendations.title: Bound
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: If A Perfect Murder clicked for you, Bound (1996) is a strong next stop: both trade in crime and thriller, with unease that lingers after the credits. Expect a different story, but a similar willingness to go big when the moment demands it.

faq.question: What should I watch after A Perfect Murder?
faq.answer: Try Bound, Killer's Kiss, Butterfly on a Wheel—each shares DNA with A Perfect Murder while changing the setting enough to feel fresh.

faq.question: What genre is A Perfect Murder?
faq.answer: A Perfect Murder (1998) is best described as Crime, Thriller, Drama. Stores and algorithms use different labels, but that mix is what fans actually recognize on a rewatch.

faq.question: Is A Perfect Murder worth revisiting?
faq.answer: Viewer scores sit around 6.5/10 on TMDB—high enough that replays are common for people who like this crime lane.
